workflow.critical: The workflow execution engine is governed by: {project-root}/_bmad/core/tasks/workflow.xml
You MUST have already loaded and processed this workflow's workflow.yaml
Communicate all responses in {communication_language} tailored to {user_skill_level}
Execute the unified next-step workflow directly from these instructions. Do not rely on an external skill file.
Ask questions when unclear - do not guess.
Validation methodology is a mandatory review gate. Reviews and implementations must use {validation_methodology_file} when available.
Single-action mode is mandatory: execute exactly ONE highest-priority action per invocation, then stop and hand off.
Never chain multiple issues/actions in one invocation. Never recurse into next-step from inside next-step.

step.action: Run `td usage --new-session` if this is a fresh context; otherwise run `td usage -q`
Run `td reviewable` and capture all issues awaiting review
Run `td ready` and capture all issues ready for implementation
Run `td status` to capture global queue health and blockers
Inspect BMAD planning artifacts to identify empty epics and epics with all tasks complete
Determine whether td execution is otherwise drained and completion validation against the PRD should run after epic maintenance
Load project context from {project_context} if present
Load validation methodology from {validation_methodology_file} if present
Infer validation context for the current issue from issue description, linked story context, changed surfaces, and validation defaults (project type, platform, requirement classes)

check.action: Mark review confidence as reduced and enforce fallback verification: run tests, lint, typecheck, build, and smoke checks where available
Add recommendation to run `/bmad:td:setup-validation` before future reviews

step.goal: Determine next action using strict priority
step.action: Apply priority in this exact order:
1) Reviews (highest): if any reviewable issues exist, choose the highest-priority reviewable item
2) Implement ready issues: if no reviews exist and ready items exist, choose the highest-priority ready item
3) Epic workflows: if no reviewable or ready items exist, run epic maintenance
For epic maintenance: if an epic has no child tasks, run BMAD create-story flow; if an epic has all tasks approved/closed, run BMAD code-review flow
4) PRD validation: if no reviewable or ready items exist and no epic maintenance remains, run BMAD td validate-prd to identify uncovered requirements and create follow-up td work

check.if: multiple candidates have equal priority and no deterministic tie-break exists
check.ask: Ask one targeted question with a recommended default tie-break, and explain outcome impact

step.action: Execute only the single action chosen in step 2. Do not select another action in this invocation.

check.critical: Review must run in a different session than implementation per td separation rules.
check.action: Load issue context, linked story context, and recent code changes
Resolve matching validation profiles using deterministic merge rules, falling back to configured default profile or legacy flat gates when needed
Apply the selected mandatory and optional validation gates from {validation_methodology_file}
For UI-impacting web or hybrid changes, require desktop/mobile screenshots plus contrast and visual stability evidence, or mark reduced confidence with explicit skip rationale
When methodology defines commands, execute them and capture pass/fail evidence
When methodology is missing, run fallback verification suite and clearly state reduced confidence
Produce machine-checkable evidence output containing validation context, changed files, gate results, artifact references, confidence, risks, and follow-ups
Produce review decision with concrete findings and required follow-ups
Submit review using td review workflow commands
Start issue with `td start {id}` and set focus
Implement only the selected issue scope
Resolve matching validation profiles using issue context before selecting required gates
Run required validation methodology checks (or fallback checks if methodology absent)
For UI-impacting web or hybrid changes, capture desktop/mobile screenshots and visual evidence before handoff, or record reduced confidence with explicit skip rationale
Do not move to review until verification evidence is recorded
Create td handoff including implementation details and machine-checkable validation evidence
Submit issue for review
For empty epics, execute BMAD create-story and synchronize td mapping/dependencies
For completed epics, execute BMAD code-review and record outcome in td
Execute BMAD td validate-prd directly from workflow files
Create or reuse td tasks for each actionable missing or partial PRD requirement
Record which requirements are fully covered, which gaps were converted into td work, and whether any residual risk remains

step.goal: Commit when applicable and report next action

check.if: code or docs changed as part of execution
check.action: Create git commit using the module commit-message format and include td references

step.action: Report what action was executed, which priority branch it came from, which validation context and profiles were applied, and what is next
End the invocation immediately after the report so autopilot can start a fresh next-step iteration.
If validation methodology was absent or weak, recommend `/bmad:td:setup-validation`
